title: Causal (Masked) Self-Attention
description:
Implement scaled dot-product self-attention with a CAUSAL mask, as used in autoregressive (decoder-only) transformers, for $Q, K, V \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times d}$ (sequence length $n$, head dim $d$). Position $i$ may only attend to positions $j \le i$:
$$\text{scores}_{i,j} = \frac{(QK^\top)_{i,j}}{\sqrt{d}}, \qquad \text{scores}_{i,j} \leftarrow -\infty \;\text{ if } j > i$$
$$\text{Attention}(Q,K,V) = \text{softmax}(\text{scores})\,V$$

Set the scores for future positions ($j > i$) to $-\infty$ BEFORE the softmax (so their softmax weight is exactly 0), then take a row-wise stable softmax over the key axis.

Implement `causal_self_attention(Q, K, V)` returning the attention output of shape `(n, d)`.

**Shapes**: `Q`, `K`, `V`: `(5, 8)` (seq_len=5, d=8) -> returns `(5, 8)`.

    pass
reference:
import numpy as np

def causal_self_attention(Q, K, V):
    Q = np.asarray(Q, dtype=np.float64)
    K = np.asarray(K, dtype=np.float64)
    V = np.asarray(V, dtype=np.float64)
    n, d = Q.shape
    scores = (Q @ K.T) / np.sqrt(d)
    mask = np.triu(np.ones((n, n), dtype=bool), k=1)
    scores = np.where(mask, -np.inf, scores)
    scores = scores - scores.max(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
    e = np.exp(scores)
    weights = e / e.sum(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
    return weights @ V
hints: Build an (n, n) boolean mask that is True where column index > row index (np.triu(..., k=1)), and set scores to -inf there before softmax so those positions get exactly zero weight.
Subtract the row max AFTER masking (not before) so the max is computed only over the allowed (unmasked) entries; rows with -inf entries otherwise stay numerically stable since exp(-inf)=0.

solutions.name: Masked scaled dot-product attention
solutions.explanation: The causal mask enforces the autoregressive constraint that position $i$ cannot see future positions $j>i$. Setting those scores to $-\infty$ before softmax makes $e^{-\infty}=0$, so masked positions receive exactly zero attention weight while the remaining weights still form a valid probability distribution over allowed positions.
solutions.code:
import numpy as np
def causal_self_attention(Q, K, V):
    Q = np.asarray(Q, dtype=np.float64)
    K = np.asarray(K, dtype=np.float64)
    V = np.asarray(V, dtype=np.float64)
    n, d = Q.shape
    scores = (Q @ K.T) / np.sqrt(d)
    mask = np.triu(np.ones((n, n), dtype=bool), k=1)
    scores = np.where(mask, -np.inf, scores)
    scores = scores - scores.max(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
    e = np.exp(scores)
    weights = e / e.sum(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
    return weights @ V
